provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the National Assembly (Salaries and Allowances) Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Salaries and allowances of Speaker and Deputy Speaker
provisions.content:
3. Salaries and allowances of Speaker and Deputy Speaker
(1) The Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Assembly shall each be paid, from moneys appropriated by law for such purpose, the appropriate salary payable in terms of section 4.
(2) The Speaker and Deputy Speaker shall, in addition, receive the allowances specified in paragraphs 3 and 8 of the Schedule and shall be eligible to receive—
( a ) for the Speaker, the allowance specified under paragraph 2(2) of the Schedule; and
( b ) for the Deputy Speaker, the allowance payable under paragraph 2(3) of the Schedule
[6 of 2019, s. 2.]

provisions.title: Salaries and allowances of Members
provisions.content:
4. Salaries and allowances of Members
(1) Members shall be paid, from moneys appropriated by law for such purpose, the salaries and allowances prescribed in the Schedule.
[16 of 2001, s. 2.]
(2) The Speaker, Deputy Speaker and each Member shall be deemed to have had incorporated into their basic salary, prescribed in the Schedule and payable over the Session of Parliament whose life commenced on the 16th October, 1999, a car subsidy allowance amounting to P120,000.
[16 of 2001, s. 2.]

provisions.title: Allowances exempt from income tax
provisions.content:
5. Allowances exempt from income tax
The allowances payable or benefits granted in terms of this Act shall be exempt from income tax.

provisions.title: Saving in respect of other allowances
provisions.content: 6. Saving in respect of other allowances

definitions.definition: the person designated by the Speaker of the Assembly as Leader of the Opposition
assigned to it under section 58 of the Constitution.
